---
title: "W06-Reflection Essay-Introduction to Positron"
author: "Lucynda Young"
date: "`r format(Sys.time())`"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 4
    toc-expand: 3
    number-sections: true
    code-link: true
    embed-resources: true
    theme: cosmo
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-tools: true
editor: visual
execute:
  freeze: auto
---

# Introduction

This reflection documents my experience using Positron...
---

# Introduction

This reflection documents my experience installing and using **Positron**, exploring AI integration, and publishing a Quarto report to GitHub Pages.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 1. Project Setup in Positron

## Prompt

> Start a new project and Quarto file (.qmd) inside Positron.\
> Create a folder called "Reflection-assignments" and organize the file structure properly.\
> Start a new project by opening that folder.\
> Create and save the qmd file with the required YAML header. \## Response

I created a folder titled **Reflection-assignments** inside my course directory using Finder on my MacBook. I then opened Positron and selected **Open Folder**, choosing the Intro-Positron folder. This automatically generated the `.posit` folder.

Creating a project folder ensures: - Reproducibility\
- Organized file management\
- Easy GitHub publishing\
- Clean project environment

::: callout-tip
Opening the folder itself (instead of just a file) ensures Positron recognizes it as a project.
:::

---
# 2. Preparing the HTML Report Using Quarto

## Prompt

> Implement as many HTML features and effects as possible.

## Response

This report demonstrates the following Quarto features:

- Table of Contents  
- Section numbering  
- Tabsets  
- Cross-referencing  
- Callout blocks  
- Hidden code  
- Code folding  
- Code overflow wrapping  
- Footnotes  
- Figures with labels  
- Tables generated with R  
---

## Example: R Code Execution

```{r}
# Simple calculation example
mean(cars$speed)

#| echo: false
summary(cars)

#| label: fig-speed
#| fig-cap: "Speed vs Stopping Distance"
plot(cars$speed, cars$dist)
```

Panel Tabset Example

::: panel-tabset
Positron Advantages

Built-in AI tools

Modern interface

Seamless Quarto integration

Faster workflow

RStudio Comparison

Traditional layout

Mature ecosystem

Requires external AI plugins
:::

Blockquote Example

> Positron integrates AI directly into the IDE, making coding more interactive and efficient.

Footnote Example

# Essay Reflection 3.1 Installing and Exploring Positron Prompt

| Download and install Positron. Follow the videos in Step 1 and Step 3.

Response

-   After downloading Positron, I followed along with the tutorial videos. I practiced:

-   Creating projects

-   Running R code

-   Rendering Quarto files

-   Exploring AI tools

-   The interface felt cleaner and more modern than RStudio.

# 3.2 What I Like About Positron Compared with RStudio Prompt

| What do you like about Positron compared with RStudio?

Response

Compared to RStudio, I prefer Positron because:

-   AI is integrated directly into the IDE

-   The interface feels less cluttered

-   Quarto rendering is seamless

-   Publishing workflows are streamlined

-   RStudio feels stable and familiar, but Positron feels designed for modern AI-supported workflows.

-   

    # AI Integration in Positron {#sec-ai} Prompt

| Describe how AI can be used inside Positron.

Response

AI inside Positron can assist with:

-   Code completion

-   Code explanation

-   Debugging

-   Writing documentation

-   Generating Quarto content

-   Refactoring code

-   Some AI features are free (limited tier), while others require subscription services.

# GitHub Copilot Experience

I applied for the GitHub Copilot Education plan and waiting to be approved.

![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Cynyng/Reflection-Assignments/refs/heads/main/Screenshot.png)

GitHub Copilot has been helpful for:

Autocompleting repetitive code

Generating function templates

Speeding up documentation writing

However, I noticed that over-reliance could reduce deep thinking. When used intentionally, it enhances productivity without replacing critical thinking.

::: callout-warning
AI should support learning, not replace analytical reasoning.
:::

Overall, I found GitHub Copilot helpful rather than distracting.

# Publishing to GitHub Pages Prompt

| Publish this report to GitHub Pages and provide a URL.

Response

To publish this report, I:

-   Created a GitHub repository

-   Pushed my .qmd and .html files

-   Enabled GitHub Pages in repository settings

-   Selected the main branch

My GitHub Pages URL:

https://github.com/Cynyng/Reflection-Assignments/

GitHub Pages hosts rendered HTML files, while GitHub repositories store source code.

# Conclusion

This module strengthened my ability to:

Create organized Positron projects

Write professional Quarto reports

Integrate AI into coding workflows

Publish content using GitHub Pages

I now feel confident using Positron as a modern data science and publishing environment.
